text: Unit tests check individual parts of your code, like a single function or method. They are fast and help you find problems early. Integration tests check how those parts work together. For example, they might test if a database and a form work correctly as a system. Test-driven development means writing the test before the code itself. This ensures your code does exactly what you expect. Together, these practices give you high confidence. When you make changes, you can run the tests quickly. If everything passes, you know the system still behaves as intended, which saves time and reduces stress.
